desc:
You are standing on the trail to the Misty Mountains. The trail runs in a
north-south direction bordered by thick bushes on both sides. The trail looks as
if it is not used now as much as it once was. To the east a high hill can
clearly be seen high over the rest of the forest.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
The plains surrounding the trail look very quiet and safe. Small bushes are
scattered all over the fields. Further to the east you see a high hill rising
over the tree-tops of the dense forest between the fields and the hill. The
trail continues in a north-south direction.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
The path continues over the plains, but further north the plains switch to small
hills with dense vegetation. To the west you see a field with small scattered
bushes, and it strikes your mind what a perfect place this would have been to
play hide and seek as a child. To the east the plains continue but not very far,
dense forests take their place as the ground climbs up a high hill in the east.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
The trail continues its way to its final goal. The trail looks like it is not
used as much now as it once was, most of the tracks left in the ground are from
the local wildlife. To the south the woods get lighter while to the north the
terrain only gets harsher.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
You are standing on the trail leading to the mountains in the east. The trail
bends continuously to avoid some of the minor hills in the area, making the
journey easier. The wildlife in the surroundings is probably rich, ranging from
different herbivores to wolves roaming the woods in the search for prey.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
Standing on the trail leading towards the mountains in the northeast you
suddenly notice a swamp just north of here. On the other side of the swamp you
see water, probably a river. The trail continues through its many twists and
turns towards its final goal, wherever that might be.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
The trail continues through many bends and turns in its general direction to the
northeast and southwest. The trail is very old, and clearly is not used very
much today. The woods surrounding the trail are quite dense right here, making
travel outside of the trail very much of an effort.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
The trail continues through the woods. To the north the ground slopes down to a
river, and further up that river you see what seems to be a bridge. The forest
gets lighter the closer to the river you get, and further west of here it ends
completely to leave place for a small swamp.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
To the north the trail aproaches a bridge, inviting you to cross the river with
ease. The trail also continues to the southwest, away from the mountains in the
east. The river runs very gently under the bridge, and west of the bridge there
is another river coming down from the north.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
The river gently flows under the bridge from the mountains in the east and just
to the west of here you see another branch of the river coming from from the
north. The bridge itself is not very large, and seems to be very old. From the
woods surrounding the path an occasional howl can be heard.
name: Bridge over the River

desc:
This seems to be a seldom trodden trail leading north and south. To the south
there is a bridge. Southwest two branches of the river Bruinen meets and
continues westward toward Rivendell.
name: Old Trail

desc:
The trail is slightly sloping upwards in the eastern direction. You can follow
the trail south and cross a river, or you can follow it east towards the Misty
Mountains, but there is shallow water north of you.
name: Old Trail

desc:
You find yourself on an old trail, between the Misty Mountains and Rivendell.
There are several paths into the Misty Mountains, but most have bad ends or will
take you to nowhere. This trail might lead into the mountains to the High Pass.
name: Old Trail

desc:
The trail leads eastward towards the Misty Mountains and hopefully to the High
Pass. The High Pass has long been an important trade route across the mountains.
It has been believed safe from orcs. Now, however, rumours say that orcs are in
the area. Still, many cross the mountains yet. It is said that Beornings from
the Vale of Anduin are keeping the pass open.
name: Old Trail

desc:
The blue water of the Bruinen floats west here with clucking and splashing
sounds. Looking south, you see a slope leading up on a plateau to the west. You
feel like getting ashore at once, and climb up there to admire the view that
ought to be really astonishing from up there. To the west, the river seems to
pick up speed on it's way west.
name: The South Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
It is very silent here, even the birds are quiet. The southern bank looks
suitable for disembarking your craft. The northern bank, wait... Wasn't that an
elf vanishing behind a large tree? Hmm, why are the birds still so quiet, you
wonder as you drift down the river.
name: The South Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
The river is making a smooth bend here, to the west. The south bank is covered
with reed, and from somewhere the croaking of a frog can softly be heard.
name: The South Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
You are floating in the middle of a calm river. It is very peaceful here. Near
the bank is a flock of resting wild ducks, gently bobbing up and down, as the
water-ripples from your vessel passes them.
name: The South Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
You are in the middle of the southern fork of the River Bruinen. The river runs
very calm and gentle here, and along the slightly muddy banks there are some
light woods.
name: South Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
You are in the middle of the southern fork of the River Bruinen. The river is
very wide here, and the southern bank is more of a swamp than solid ground. To
the north you see a dark pine-forest. To the east the river makes some gentle
bends and turns, but continues upstream in an eastern direction.
name: South Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
You are in the southern fork of the River Bruinen, which slowly makes it way
westwards. East of here you see another branch of the river coming down from the
north. Further east there is a bridge over the eastern branch. The southern bank
looks to be very wet and swamp-like ground.
name: South Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
You are in the southern fork of the River Bruinen. From the north another branch
comes down, and joins together with the branch coming from the east. Across the
eastern branch there is a small stone-bridge, and a path to the north and south
of the bridge. Further west on the southern bank the ground seem to be very wet,
almost like a swamp.
name: South Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
You are in the middle of the river. Just to the west of here you see a path
going across the river on a stone-bridge. Further to the east the river has its
source somewhere in the Mountains. On the northern side of river the bank is
much more gentle than the somewhat rockier southern bank.
name: South Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
You are in the middle of the southern fork of the River Bruinen. Far to the west
you see a bridge going across the river. Further to the east the river seem to
be much wilder as it exits out of a ravine. The southern bank is very rocky, and
further east it becomes even impossible to climb up the southern bank.
name: South Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
You are in a branch of the river. To the east the river bends to the south as it
exits out of a steep ravine. To the south you have huge cliffs, preventing you
from exit the river that way. To the north the bank is much nicer, but even that
bank becomes more rocky to the east. Far to the east you see a bridge across the
river.
name: South Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
You are in the river just at the place where it bends to the west, out of the
ravine to the south. The water is very fast here, and you have to work hard to
stay put and not be pulled away by the strong current. North of here the bank is
very rocky, but still climbable.
name: South Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
You are in a fast flowing part of the river as it passes through a steep ravine.
The cliffs on both sides are very high and rough, making it impossible to exit
the river here. The current is very strong here, so it would be impossible to go
upstream, the only way to go is following the current downstream, out of the
ravine to the north.
name: River in a Ravine

desc:
The current in the river becomes stronger and stronger the closer you get to the
ravine to the west. The ravine is very steep, and once you enter it will
probably be impossible to go against the current upstream again. To the south
there is a light forest that continues almost all the way down to the river.
name: South Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
This forest is very light, and follows the southern bank of the river just north
of here. Looking westwards along the river you see the river entering a ravine.
A steep slope goes up to the top of the ravine, and to the south the forest
continues and gets darker and darker the further in you go.
name: Light Forest

desc:
Surrounded by high trees you get a feeling that you are very small in this
world. To the south the woods get more and more dense. But blocking your way is
a steep cliff. Far away in a northern direction you hear water splashing agains
rocks with full force, it might be the sound of a waterfall.
name: Light Woods

desc:
The trees surrounding you are tall and magnificent, the further to the south you
get the thicker they grow. So far it is a quite open forest, but to the south of
here a thick wall of bushes seems to hinder your way. To the north you see a
slope going up to a high plateau. Far away you can hear the sound of rushing
water.
name: Light Woods

desc:
You are standing on a quite steep and rocky slope, going up to a plateau
overlooking the ravine that forms to the north of you. Further down the slope
enters the light forest surrounding the cliffs. To the northeast of here the
ravine ends, and the river continues in a gentle manner upstream.
name: Rocky Slope

desc:
You are standing on top of a huge cliff overlooking the ravine through which a
river makes its way down in a violent manner. The river is very rapid, and it
would probably prove impossible to even try to go upstream through the ravine.
To the south the plateau continues and to the west the cliffs along the river
gradually become lower and lower.
name: Overlooking the Ravine

desc:
This plateau is on the top of some huge cliffs overlooking a narrow ravine
below. Through the ravine you see a rapid stream making its way down to the exit
of the ravine further to the north. To the east the ground slopes down to a
light forest at the bank of the river. To the south and west steep cliffs
prevent you from going down the plateau.
name: Rocky Plateau

desc:
The banks become more and more rocky the further east you go. Just to the east
of here they grow into huge and steep cliffs, making it impossible to reach down
to the river below. To the east you hear a loud noise, as if there was a
waterfall or some rapids.
name: Rocky Riverbank

desc:
This bank of the river is a little more rocky than the smoother northern bank.
Further to the east the rocks become larger and the bank slowly develops into
high cliffs far to the east. North of here the river flows quite gently and
passes under a bridge that leads a trail across the water just to the west.
name: Riverbank

desc:
You are in the middle of a forest with high trees. On the ground there grow some
bushes with some black berries on them. To the south you see what seem to be
some hills. To the north huge, sharp rocks and steep cliffs make it nearly
impossible to walk.
name: Tall Woods

desc:
To the south these woods with high trees change into a very hilly landscape, and
further to the south you see the hills grow even higher. North and east huge
rocks and cliffs stop your way, especially to the east where the cliffs are very
steep. To the west however the forest continues, and some bushes with berries on
them grow on the ground.
name: Tall Woods

desc:
This is the northern part of a range of hills that continues to the south. East
of here the hills descend down to the bottom of a high cliff that reaches high
into the sky. North of here a forest with high and slender trees begins.
name: High Hills

desc:
Surrounding you on the northern, southern and eastern side are high cliffs,
reaching for the sky above, making you feel very small in this world. To the
west a range of hills rises high into the sky, and to the southwest you see a
high peak rise above all the others. The ground here is covered with huge rocks
that probably have fallen down from the cliffs above.
name: Under the High Cliffs

desc:
The forest is very dense here, to the west it is so thick it is impossible to
even go there. East of here a high hill rises high above the surrounding woods.
From a distant place you can hear wolves howl, perhaps they found your tracks
and decided they are hungry.
name: Dense Woods

desc:
You are in a dense forest with high trees completely surrounding you making
travel here very hard unless you follow the small natural paths that are
scattered around in the woods. To the east you see a high hill rising above
everything else in the area.
name: Dense Woods

desc:
The woods surrounding you are truly dense, even though they open up somewhat to
the east as the ground ascends up on the hillside. A little further north, among
the hills, you see the high peak that can be seen from a very long distance.
name: Dense Woods

desc:
You are at the border of a dense forest surrounding the high hill to the east.
Just west of here the forests open up to a slope that descends to the plains
below. To the north the forest is so thick that it is impossible to go there. On
the ground you see lots of tracks of different animals, it is probably safe to
say there is much wildlife in this forest.
name: Dense Woods

desc:
The tall grass growing on the plain is thick and waves back and forth in the
wind, making it look like an ocean. The plain slopes down from the forests in
the east to the lower ground in the west where you can see a trail make its way
north to the mountains. Rising high over the tree-tops you see a high hill not
far to the east.
name: Sloping Plains

desc:
You are standing on a small plain sloping down to the trail from the dense
forests above. The slope is not very steep, just a small gentle one. The grass
growing here is quite tall, and here and there you can even find a small tree
inviting you to sit down and take a rest.
name: Sloping Plains

desc:
The ground suddenly slopes down to the west and the dense forest from the east
suddenly opens up in a light forest. The ground is also covered with light
bushes and the flowers grow wild during summer. This is probably also a forest
where lots of herbivores find their food as the supply of grass and bushes is so
great.
name: Lightly Forested Slope

desc:
The forest seems to somewhat open up to the west as the ground starts to slope
down a little. To the east, however, the forest seems to grow even thicker and
the undervegetation becomes more sparse. Far to the northeast you see some high
hills; the view from up there would probably be magnificent.
name: Dense Woods

desc:
The dense woods around you are making the travel hard and boring. Sometimes the
quiet and silent surrounding is disturbed by a howl from a wolf or some bird
landing in a tree-top above you.
name: Dense Woods

desc:
The woods seem to go on forever with the small exception of some high hills
starting just northeast of here. The trees in the surrounding area are tall and
have many thick branches, making the sunlight very dim. On the ground there are
some fallen trees that are very murky and rotten.
name: Dense Woods

desc:
The ground start to rise here, starting off with very small hills but rapidly
growing into higher ones. The forest growing on the slopes is quite dense and
there is a clear lack of sun to the ground. Westwards the forest seems to thin
out, but not very much.
name: Rising Hills

desc:
The forest opens up somewhat as the hill ascends over the treetops of the forest
below. Further east the hills get even higher, and the forest on their top is
very scarce. West of the forest you see fields that never seem to end, and a
track running in a north-south direction.
name: High Hills

desc:
The woods on the northern side of the hills are not as thick as the ones further
to the west. To the south the hills grow higher, and in their middle you see a
high hill rise above the others. The forest continues to the north and west. The
ground-vegetation here is quite sparse, and here and there you see some
rabbit-holes indicating that there might be rabbits around.
name: Tall Woods

desc:
You are now standing on the highest hill in the area; it rises high above the
others and the view from here is magnificent. To the east you see the Misty
Mountains tower up like a giant wall. Westwards the forest opens up in wide
plains, and to the north you see parts of the River Bruinen making its way to
the southwest.
name: Top of a High Hill

desc:
The landscape around you has changed very dramatically compared to the wide
forests around the hills. The hills are covered with a sparse forest, some
plains with high bushes and high grass. To the north the hills grow even higher,
culminating in a high hill that can be seen from very far away. To the east the
hills slope down in a very thick and dark forest.
name: High Hills

desc:
The forest surrounding you is quite dense and it seems to continue to the east
and west. North of here the ground rises up in some hills where the vegetation
clears up a little. To the south the forest also clears up a little, and you see
a small pond in the forest.
name: Dense Woods

desc:
The forest slowly changes and becomes thicker and thicker the further east you
go. East of here the forest has so thick undergrowth that it is not even worth
considering going there. To the north the dark forest continues, but to the
south and west it seems to clear up a little, making it possible for the
sunlight to reach the ground.
name: Edge of a Dark Forest

desc:
West of here the hills seem to go on forever, to the east however they fade and
leave place to a dark and cold forest. The slopes of the last hills are covered
with light bushes, providing some shelter for all the animals living in the
area.
name: Edge of a Dark Forest

desc:
The vegetation on these hills changes dramatically with respect to that of the
other hills in the area. Gone are the scattered bushes and the sparse forest.
Instead here grow thick, thorny bushes that would probably tear you apart if you
tried to force your way through them. To the west the hills grow higher, and you
see a hill that rises above the others like the crown of the hills.
name: High Hills

desc:
The forest here is very dark and it is very hard to travel anywhere, in fact it
is impossible to travel anywhere but west from here as the thick bushes and
trees effectively block your way. The ground has no vegetation whatsoever, just
plain soil surrounding the thick roots of the trees and bushes.
name: Deep inside the Dark Forest

desc:
As the forest continues it gets darker and darker all the time, thick bushes
with lots of sharp thorns get more and more frequent, making travel here very
tiresome. To the west the bushes seem to clear up some though, and the forest
seems to get a little lighter, even though that does not say much.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
You are amid some very thick bushes, just at the face of a cliff to the east.
The sunlight probably never comes all the way down under this bush. North of the
bushes there is a light forest, but to the south the forest is much thicker and
darker.
name: Under the Thick Bushes

desc:
The dark forest seems to go on forever but to the north you see something out of
the ordinary, a huge cliff rising quite high up. At the bottom of the cliff
there are some huge bushes, it seems to be impossible to force your way through.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
Thick bushes and trees make up the main vegetation here. The bushes are indeed
powerful and their roots are big and bulky. To the east the forest grows even
thicker and it is probably not possible to travel far in that direction. South
of here the forest cleans up somewhat, making that the only way out of this
gloomy place.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
Walking in the light vegetation close to the road makes you forget about all the
dangers in the forests around you. You hear birds singing from the bushes in the
area. North of here the bushy landscape continues, but to the east and on the
other side of the path the forest has completely taken over.
name: Light Vegetation

desc:
You have obviously stumbled into the lair of a bear. You see skeletons of small
animals lying around, and you can almost feel the large inhabitant breathing you
in the neck. Perhaps it would be best to get out of here really soon... The only
way out is west, back through the cave opening.
name: A Dark Cave

desc:
To the west, there is a hill, that might give you a nice glance around the
surrounding land. Rocks and thick foliage prevents you from going further east.
To the south, there is a small grove, from which you hear the beautiful song of
some birds.
name: East of a Hill

desc:
Some small trees have gathered here, in the middle of nowhere. Small birds are
singing, and you hear the twigs moving as the small animals rush around on the
ground. This small grove is in the middle of the vast plains continuing to the
west. To the east you can see light vegetation. To the north, you see a small
field, just next to a hill.
name: Dense Grove

desc:
You have entered a small cave hidden behind a large boulder. The cave is not
very big, and on the ground you see remains of what once was some animal. The
conclusion is that this cave is inhabited by some flesh-eater. To the south the
cave-entrance leads back out into the open vegetation.
name: Small Cave

desc:
You are in a quite flat area with an occasional small hill here and there.
Scattered bushes grow all over the place, but not very dense. To the north,
behind one of the bushes, you see a boulder that looks like it has a cavity
behind it. East of here a path runs in a north-south direction.
name: Light Vegetation

desc:
You have reached the top of a small hill overlooking the river as it bends
around the hill from the east to the south. The river runs very quietly down
there and you see birds floating around there, and an occasional fish splash in
the water. To the east the hill descends into a minor swamp.
name: Hill at the Bank of the River

desc:
You are on the southern bank of the river. The area is more of a swamp than
solid ground. To the west the swamp ends as the ground rises into a small hill
overlooking the river. South of here the swamp slowly changes into a light
forest.
name: Southern Bank

desc:
To the north-east two forks of the river join together, pushing the water
towards the southern bank. This is probably the main reason why you are having
problems finding solid ground to stand on. Further up the banks the tree-line
marks the beginning of solid ground, and from within the forest you heard birds
singing which, combined with the gentle flow of the river, gives you a true
nature-experience.
name: Southern Bank

desc:
This is a small clearing in the forest. In every direction except north you see
thick, thorny bushes which make it impossible to walk that way without an
enormous amount of trouble. To the north there is some light forest separating
the clearing from the southern bank of the river.
name: Small Clearing

desc:
You are standing in short grass, barely reaching above your boots. If you have a
vessel, you can go into the river Bruinen just north of here. To the west, you
see a peaceful spot, very suitable for taking a break. You see a small hill in
the south-west.
name: Grass

desc:
This calm spot next to the steady river to the north seems to be an excellent
place to rest, and recover from the hazardous travels through this unfriendly
land. To the east you see a grassy field, and to the south, you can climb a
small hill.
name: At the Riverside

desc:
From this small hill, you get an overview of the nearest surroundings. To the
north, you see the river Bruinen. It is yet not the wide, mighty river it will
become as it reaches its mouth at the Mighty Ocean in the far west, but it still
gives a mighty impression as it floats by. To the west, you see a small spring
with a lodge south of it.
name: On the Hill

desc:
Aha... Finally, a trace of human(oid?) life out here. To the north, you see the
back of a small lodge. Vast plains are to the south and the west. East, passage
is blocked by thick bushes. Listening carefully, you hear the sound of running
water from the north.
name: Vast Plains

desc:
Well, well... Still on these old plains. Looking south, you see a small change
in the land though - the plains seem to become more and more rocky there. In all
other directions you see nothing but plains. Far in the east, the Hithaeglir, as
the elves call the Misty Mountains tower above the land... In their shadow is a
small but dense grove of trees.
name: Vast Plains

desc:
You seem to have reached the southern end of the vast plains. Dense bushes and
some trees make progress impossible to the south. Vast plains stretch east,
north, and west. To the east, the ground appears to be covered with rocks,
making passage harder. Looking across the plains, you see some form of motion,
not sure whether it is man or beast... or something else.
name: Vast Plains

desc:
You are under the stone-bridge across the fork of the river. The path above you
runs in a north-south direction. At the southern base of the bridge you see a
small hole in the stonewall. To the west you see a smaller river coming down
from the north, merging with this branch.
name: Under the Bridge

desc:
You are now so far within the forest that your sense of direction is fading.
There is absolutely no light from the sun this deep within the forest, but you
can still follow the path. There are a few paw-prints in the dried dirt, about
the size of a large dog's foot.
name: Deep within the Wild Forest

desc:
The tracks in the dirt become more well defined, as you follow the trail into
the deepest parts of the forest. Any resemblance of a peaceful forest are lost
now, and a definite sense of evil has overcome the area. In the distance you can
hear wolves howling. The trail takes a turn here, going from the west to the
north.
name: Deep within the Wild Forest

desc:
As you enter this small house, you notice how dirty it is. There are rags thrown
about everywhere, a layer of dust on all the furniture, and a big pile of dirty
dishes near the fire. A few cots line the far wall, and they aren't even made.
You get the feeling that whatever lives here doesn't even have a mother to watch
out for them.
name: Troll's Hideout

desc:
An ancient name for this dark pine forest, which stretches itself northward, is
Taur-en-Morthonion. During day light can be seen to the south, and the sound of
running water comes from that direction.
name: Pine Forest

desc:
You have reached the southeastern corner of the forest. The river runs east and
south of you. You also notice that the river branches here. There is a bridge
across one of the branches, but you will have to cross the other branch to get
there.
name: Corner of a Forest

desc:
This is deep inside a pine forest. It is as dark as the darkest night. The
branches of huge trees around you let almost no light through. The ancient name
of this forest is Taur-en-Morthonion, the Forest of Dark Pines.
name: Dark Pine Forest

desc:
In this part of the forest is dominated by younger trees. They are tall and with
thick trunks, but the lowest branches are in reach of your hands. You find a
climbable tall tree. If you climb up, you may get an overlook of the forest.
name: Forest

desc:
The braying of wolves is louder now, and it seems that you have invaded the
territory of something dangerous. The wolf tracks lead to the north, but there
are more tracks that lead to the south, and end abruptly.
name: Deep within the Wild Forest

desc:
Now within the very bowels of the forest, a slow terror begins to creep into
your bones. You begin to lose all track of time, as the evil feeling within
grows and grows. The path is littered with tracks and takes another turn to the
east.
name: Deep within the Wild Forest

desc:
The forest takes another twist here, giving you the choice of turning back or
heading to the north. The scores on some of the trees are old, but they can
still be seen in the trees.
name: Deep within the Wild Forest

desc:
As you follow the path, you see a break in the forest to the west. There you can
see a large hill, and the sky above it. The path ends here, but you can continue
a bit more to the north.
name: Deep within the Wild Forest

desc:
You must be pretty far into the forest. There seems to be no end of it in any
direction. It is dark as the darkest night. Above you, there is a thick layer of
branches that let almost no light through. The heart of the forest might be to
the west.
name: Forest

desc:
There are dark pine trees in all directions and no path can be found. Many
travellers has been lost in this hostile enviroment. Maybe the forest's
inhabitants have something to do with it to.
name: Dark Pine Forest

desc:
A river flows silently just east of you, forming the eastern border of this dark
forest. On the other side of the river there is a trail going east into the
mountains.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
The thick layer of leaves over your head prevents any light from getting into
the forest. The trees here are as wild as any others in the forest, and there is
nothing taming them in this part of the forest.
name: Deep within the Wild Forest

desc:
The part of the forest tamed by the elven rangers is far gone now, and the trees
and undergrowth block travel in all directions except to the east and south. The
forest has a nice, peaceful feeling here.
name: Deep within the Wild Forest

desc:
The feeling of nature is very heavy in this part of the forest. These parts seem
untravelled by anything other than the lower animals. You can go either to the
north or to the west, all other ways are blocked by the foliage.
name: Deep within the Wild Forest

desc:
The roots here seem to reach up and trip you as they weave a web across the
floor of the forest. There are so many roots that you can almost walk on them
instead of the ground.
name: Deep within the Wild Forest

desc:
The sounds of nature surround you as you walk through the forest. As you get
used to walking through the forest, it becomes easier to avoid the roots and
vines. Movement is possible to the north and east.
name: Deep within the Wild Forest

desc:
The undergrowth here is a bit sparser than in other places in the forest. There
are a few large tracks visible in the dirt underneath. The trees allow movement
to east and the south.
name: Deep within the Wild Forest

desc:
As you make your way through the forest, the undergrowth tugs on your pants and
scratches up your boots. You can see that some of the undergrowth has recently
been moved and shoved up against the trees.
name: Deep within the Wild Forest

desc:
A small animal, or family of animals has burrowed into the undergrowth here.
This small place smells of wet fur and dirty animals. The ground is made up of
packed dirt and the roof and sides are dirt and some of the undergrowth that
surrounds everything in the forest. The burrow is a tight fit for you, and it
continues on to the east, getting even tighter.
name: Burrow

desc:
You start climbing up the hills here, which is not too hard, as the slope is
rather gentle and free of rocks. The hill climbs up to a peak to the west where
the view is somewhat better. To the south lies a large, sprawling forest, filled
with trees that grow close together.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
As you climb up the gentle hill, you find yourself feeling better for yourself.
You think that if you can conquer this hill, then the great Misty Mountains that
can be seen off in the distance will be as foothills to one of your abilities.
The hills lead off to the west, and the plains to the north.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
Here the trees of the forest, which lies south and west, grow sparse. Out of the
forest, a monotonous flatland lies. Thick trees block large-scale movement
southwards into the forest. There are a number of pawprints in the ground
leading from the west and ending here.
name: Outskirts of the Forest

desc:
The trees here are too thick for you to go anywhere but back to the south. The
ground is covered with the undergrowth, and walking here is harder than anywhere
else in the forest. Sticks and twigs catch at your pants scratching them up a
bit.
name: Deep within the Wild Forest

desc:
You walk among thick trunks of old pines. The ground is made of packed earth,
and nothing but the pines grow here. The lowest branches of the trees are well
above your head, and you can move quite freely around. The light is dim. Not
even on a bright day, will you be able to see much. From the depths of the
forest you hear strange sounds. Foul beasts might lurk in the shadows.
name: Forest

desc:
Huge pines stand close to each other. Most of the light never reach the ground.
Some strange sounds comes from the depths of the forest. To the west is a small
mountain.
name: Forest

desc:
Hills to north, northeast and east makes a frame to this dark forest which
stretches itself south and west. Southwest is a small mountain. Large and very
steep mountains gives the horizon to the north a cruel look.
name: Forest

desc:
Dark trees covers the flat hill. The vegetation is dense and the air is damp. A
high mountain raises to the north. Not even a very skilled climber could defeat
such hostile rocks. The mountain opens up to the east. There an overhang shades
the ground from all sunlight.
name: Dark Hill

desc:
You are in the outskirts of a very large and dark forest. Rumours say that very
large creatures inhabit this area. North and east there are smaller trees and a
less dense vegetation. The soil is better here. Southeast runs a river slowly
along the border of the forest.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
This is the northeast part of a very big dark forest. North of you is a steep
slope covered with a thick layer of snow during the winter. Then a cold wind
from Misty Mountains brings tons of snow. During the summer it never completely
melts, as it lies in the shadow of the sun most of the day.
name: Dark Pine Forest

desc:
The dense vegetation on the hill makes it hard to pass. No sunlight can be seen
during the day. The undergrowth is sparse and you hear no birds singing. The air
is damp.
name: Dark Hill

desc:
A lot of tall trees grow very dense on this flattened hill. Thick roots
penetrates the rich soil. Far north a mountain raises and shelters an east west
passage by an overhang. It is not possible to go east. The dark hills continues
south and west.
name: Dark Hill

desc:
The hostile mountain to the north becomes somewhat friendly when it shelters you
from hard winds and rain at this place. To the west is a flat hill covered with
dark trees. The overhang continues eastwards.
name: Under an Overhang

desc:
The sun never reaches this place. There are several rocks and the only
vegetation are grey lichens. The air is very damp. It is like a breath from the
mountain which, like a mouth, opens up to the north.
name: Under an Overhang

desc:
The rocky flats are shelterd from rain by an overhang. It also makes this place
permanently dark. During day some light can be seen from south and east. Some
nights sounds and fragile light comes from east.
name: Rocky Flats

desc:
This is the entrance to a large cave. This far you can ride into it but if you
want to go any further with a mount you have to dismount. A dark tunnel leads
down into the mountain.
name: Cave

desc:
The tunnel is wide and extremely steep. Carvings of men with long spears on the
walls reveal that this tunnel once was made by Hillmen. Perhaps it was once used
as a burial chamber. The tunnel continues further down.
name: Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel takes you no further down into the mountain. No matter how silent you
might try to move your steps make faint sounds. By the sound of the echo you can
assume that west of here is a large chamber.
name: Tunnel

desc:
You are in a very large chamber almost thirty feet below the surface of the
ground above. There are sound of breathing. It may have been your own.
name: In a Dark Chamber

desc:
This corner of the chamber seems to be pretty intact. Everywhere else in the
chamber, the things that were put here originally had been destroyed and old
meals and excrements of trolls cover the floor. This part, however, seems tidy.
A very interesting painting is on the western wall.
name: Deep inside a Dark Chamber

desc:
This chamber is really big, about nineteen feet across. The chamber was once
used as a burial chamber, but there are no bodies or treasures to find in this
very room.
name: Deep inside a Dark Chamber

desc:
This is a burial chamber. You found some tombs, but they are destroyed, and the
bodies that once were laid there are scattered around on the floor. Dry grass
has been lain on the floor around the tombs and appears to serve as beds for
someone who is very tall.
name: Somewhere in a Dark Chamber

desc:
A small overhang shelters this place from harsh weather. A big, burned out, camp
fire indicates some visitors. Some rudiments from a corpse can be a sign of a
meal. It looks like some kind of metal is stuck into the ground or just left
there but not totally covred with rocks and soil.
name: Camp Fire

desc:
The ground is covered by gravel and small rocks. Scattered birches are fighting
for their existence. Long ago, an avalanche caused huge amounts of snow to slide
down and destroy all in its path.
name: Large Hill

desc:
You are near the top of a hill. Another, but lower, hill is to the east. If if
go there you might not be able to come back from that direction. The sides of
this hill are rather steep.
name: Large Hill

desc:
You are close to the top of this very large hill which stretches itself south-
wards. When the sky is clear you have a nice overlook of the lands. North and
far southwest are large and dark forests. There are some flat areas and other
places are covered by hills. To the east the Misty Mountains dominate the view.
name: Large Hill

desc:
No trees grows here. The ground is swampy but easy to cross. A crevice to the
north prevents you to go to the low hill there. Eastwards a flat area opens up.
South and west are large hills.
name: Swamp

desc:
You are in a flat area south of some high hills. There are not many trees. Only
small birchwoods survive the harsh climate of this area. Towards the Misty
Mountains the hills get larger and stepper.
name: Flats

desc:
You are on the top of a cliff. Cliffs rise sheer from the water around all the
lake. The lake is one of the sources of Bruinen.
name: Cliffs

desc:
You are in the middle of a deep lake. The water is cold and dark. There are no
fish or other living creatures in the water. An extremely skilled climber can
manage to climb up on the cliffs north of here.
name: Lake

desc:
This branch of the river Bruinen joins another branch south of here. To the west
is the corner of a very large and dark forest. To the east is an old trail and
to the southeast an old bridge.
name: Branch to River Bruinen

desc:
You are on a branch of the river Bruinen, between a dark forest of pines at the
western side, and an old trail on the opposite side. The river runs silently to
the south, where it joins a larger branch of Bruinen.
name: Branch to River Bruinen

desc:
You are on a small branch of the river Bruinen. It flows to the south and its
origin is in a rocky area to the northeast. There is an old and seldom trodden
trail along the side of the river.
name: Branch to River Bruinen

desc:
You are not very far from the source of this branch of Bruinen. Water flows from
some hills to the northeast. The river is calm as it runs through a flat area.
There is an old trail south of here.
name: Shallows

desc:
You are on a branch of river Bruinen close to its source. The mountains east and
south are to steep to climb. During very dry seasons there is not much water
flowing in the river which might make this part to a shallow section. On the
sharp rocks are several bones from unfortunate climbers.
name: Branch to River Bruinen

desc:
This is the source to one of the contributing rivers of Bruinen running from
from a lake of fair size. The lake, east of here, can't be reached from this
place. The sound of a waterfall to the south is getting noisy. North and west
steep hillsides makes it impossible for mounts to go that way. During very dry
seasons there is not much water here and this part is then a shallow section of
the river.
name: Rapid Water

desc:
The water comes from a lake in the north and flows rapidly westwards and you can
hear some noise from a waterfall. A steep mountain side blocks your view to the
east. This part of the river is a shallow section during very dry seasons.
name: Rapid Water

desc:
This is a lake of fair size. It is the source of the river which runs out of its
southwestern corner. There are no streams flowing from the mountain sides into
the lake. Cliffs rise sheer up from the cold and deep water.
name: Lake

desc:
The river of Bruinen has many sources. This is one of them. Far west, near the
Ford of Bruinen, it will grow larger. Much later it will join the River of
Mitheithel and form Gwathlo, the Greyflood. At Tharbad, Glanduin joins the
river. At Lond Daer in Eriador the river finally joins the Great Sea.
name: Lake

desc:
This is the end of a dark and cold lake which supplies a branch of the river
Bruinen with fresh and clear water. To the north is a very steep mountain side.
The cliff east of here looks much easier to reach than the cliffs up from here.
name: Lake

desc:
You are on a top of a cliff. The cliff rises sheer from a lake. The lake is one
of the sources of Bruinen. One of Bruinen's contributing rivers runs from this
lake to the lands below.
name: Cliff

desc:
You manage to pull yourself through the wet tunnel. Suddenly it becomes much
wider. On a big rock are some bones. You hear a sound and there is a movement in
the shallow water.
name: Cave

desc:
All around you are scattered birches twisted into miserable forms by the wind
and winter snow. This is really an unpleasant area. The inland climate gives
cold winters and warm summers. During the summer mosquitos are a horrible
plague.
name: Birchwoods

desc:
You are east of a large hill. The hill side is too steep to climb. To get to the
top of it, you will have to walk around it searching for a better route to the
top. The area elsewhere is mostly flat, covered with scattered small birches and
a few small pines.
name: Flat Hill

desc:
The wind is really strong here. It howls through the sparse trees and your
clothes make a flapping sound. The scattered birches are waving in the wind.
name: Flats

desc:
You are in a light birchwood. The trees stand scattered and offer you little
shelter from the chilly wind. They are small and twisted by snow and wind. Some
of them are brown and they look like a good material for a weaponsmith.
name: Birchwoods

desc:
This cliff rises steep from the flat surroundings. To the south is a dark lake.
The lake is the source of one of the contributors to the Bruinen river.
name: Cliffs

desc:
There are no trees on this flat area. Strange rocks with sharp points looks like
partly fallen pillars. The wind whistles and howls as it passes between them.
name: Rocky Flats

desc:
There are no trees here, not even a single birch. Almost no soil have found a
shelter among all rocks. Only thin grass waves in the harsh wind. There are a
lot of stones in the ground.
name: Rocky Flats

desc:
The landscape is hilly and rocky. Far north and far east there are several
mountains. A large and dark forest grows southwest beyond the flat area covered
with birches. Just north of here there are rocky flats. The hills to the east
and south can easily be reached from here. The strange hill west faces this hill
with steep sides. You must first go south if you want to climb it and
investigate the formations on the top.
name: Hill

desc:
It is very dark and only some lichens can survive in this spartan enviroment.
The mountain to the north is high and very steep. Long time ago an avalanche
caused tons of snow to slide down the mountainside. It passed the overhang and
deposited rocks and trees south of here. The overhang continues east and west.
name: Under an Overhang

desc:
An overhang stretches out from the mountain in the north and almost touches a
smaller mountain in the south. It is very dark. From a big crack in the southern
mountain some sounds can be heard.
name: Under an Overhang

desc:
There are some mysterious stones on the top. They once formed a thick wall. Who
would build anything in the wild land, so far away from all towns and villages?
This land was once a part of the kingdom of Rhudaur. It was habited by Hill-men.
Then the area was deserted after the defeat of the Witch-king of Angmar more
than thousand years ago. The ruins might be remnants from that distant time.
name: Old Wall

desc:
You finally reached the top of the hill and start examining the ruins. There is
not much left of what once stood here, and you ponder a while trying to
understand its purpose. When you search the ground you find broken parts of
bones. You start digging and reveal more. You find larger bones, and skulls.
Human skulls! You realise that this once has been a kind of shrine, a place of
sacrificing. The sacrifices have been men mostly, but you even find some bones
of small children.
name: Ruins

desc:
You are in a flat area covered with a light birchwood. The flats expand far to
the east, to the foot of the Misty Mountains. You see the mighty peaks of the
Mountains rise sheer up into sky. Long tails of clouds are hanging from the
peaks as the wind is forced up the steep sides of the mountains, bringing much
snow as well, also forming glaciers in areas sheltered from the sun.
name: Birches

desc:
Just a few birches and some scattered grass found its home on this rocky hill.
This is not a nice place to stay at when the wind blows.
name: Rocky Hill

desc:
The area is covered by a light birch forest. The trees are smaller than those
which can be found in southern areas where the climate isn't so harsh. Long
winters and strong winds rule here.
name: Birches

desc:
This flat area is covered with a light birchwood. The trees do not look like the
brethil found in southern areas. These are much smaller, nine feet at most.
Strong wind and snow during the winter have given them twisted forms. Some lie
almost horizontally. They continue to the north and to the east. From the south
the sound of running water can be heard.
name: Birches

desc:
The birches in the light birchwood are smaller than the ordinary ones that are
found in southern areas. Tall mountain peaks above the eastern horizon remind
you that you are close to the Misty Mountains. Most paths into those mountains
have bad ends. If your destination is those mountains, choose your path
carefully.
name: Birches

desc:
There are rocks on the ground. The humid air makes it impossible to see anything
else. The water is loud and nothing else can be heard. Beware, from time to
time, when there is a lot of water, no magic can be used here. This part of the
river is only shallow when there is not much water during very dry seasons.
name: Below a Waterfall

desc:
You are on a small mountain along a river. Huge boulders and rocks have to be
traversed. The nearby waterfall make the air thick and humid. A rumbling sound
can be heard.
name: Mountain

desc:
There are five doors in this corridor, just like the painting on the wall in the
chamber above. Maybe Hill-men built the chamber during the time they were loyal
to the Witch-king of Angmar. There is an inscription above the entrance to this
corridor. Probably because of the domination of the Witch-king, the author had
tried to write it in the Black Speech. One does not need to be an expert in
languages to see that the grammar is really bad. Probably it is supposed to say
that five chieftains of Hill-men are buried here. They were all buried in 1974,
slain in great battles the year before the defeat of the Witch-king and the fall
of Rhudaur.
name: The Corridor

desc:
You stand in the middle of a corridor. There are five doors, three doors to the
north of the corridor, and two to the south. Each door opens into a small
chamber, each with a tomb of a chieftain of Hill-men.
name: The Corridor

desc:
You are in the end of the corridor. Five doors, three north, two south are set
into the walls of the corridor. Each door is a door into a small chamber, each
containing the tomb of a chieftain of Hill-men.
name: The Corridor

desc:
You are now in the chamber of a chieftain named 'Ghar-Gleh'. His name is carved
into the northern wall. More than a thousand years ago, he was a chieftain of
the Hill-men.
name: Chamber of 'Ghar-Gleh'

desc:
This chamber contains the tomb of 'Ghar-Bledh', a chieftain among Hill-men in
this area more than a thousand years ago. His name is written on the northern
wall.
name: Chamber of 'Ghar-Bledh'

desc:
This chamber contains the tomb of a chieftain named 'Ghar-Bidch'. That name is
written on the northern wall. It is written with ordinary Tengwar, but the
language is unknown. Most likely, it is written in the language the Hill-men
spoke more than a thousand years ago.
name: Chamber of 'Ghar-Bidch'

desc:
The air of this hidden staircase is musky and reeks of disuse. There are no
tracks of the troll's clumsy feet in the dust on the floor. No offal or refuse
from their inhabitation touches this place. Perhaps this will lead you to the
chambers that were mentioned in the painting in the large chamber.
name: Staircase

desc:
This chamber contains the tomb of a chieftain named 'Ghar-Dehn'. His name is
written on the southern wall. It is written with Tengwar, but you don't
recognise the language. Most likely, it is written in the language spoken among
the Hill-men in these areas more than thousand years ago.
name: Chamber of 'Ghar-Dehn'

desc:
This is the chamber where the tomb of 'Ghar-Adje'. He was once a chieftain of
the Hill-men, and probably slain fighting the Dunedain in a battle a long time
ago. His name is written on the southern wall.
name: Chamber of 'Ghar-Adje'

desc:
A sudden silence fills the cave as you enter. There is a lot of dust in the
thick air. This is not a resting place for any creature who has some sanity
left.
name: Cave

desc:
Among the fallen stones you found a hole, and you entered it and reached the
bottom of a narrow tunnel. From west a calm sound enters this humid place. It
almost sounds like someone singing but it is ice in the voice.
name: Cave

desc:
You are on the northern riverbank next to a bridge. The trail north of you bends
and continues on the other side of the river. Behind the bridge, you see that
the river is joined by a smaller river.
name: Riverbank

desc:
You are on the northern side of a river running from the east to the west. The
ground is covered with sand and it is obvious that the river is often much
wider. Its source is up in the mountains. The borders of the bed indicate the
river contains different amounts of water, probably more after rainy periods or
during the snow-metling season.
name: Riverbank

desc:
You walk on a sandy riverbank. The river flows silently to west, and far away,
it appears to run into a deep valley. North of you is an old trail going into
the Mountains.
name: Riverbank

desc:
A river is south of you. It runs silently to the west, and you see that it is
joined by another small river far away. You also notice a bridge across the
river. If you follow the trail north of you, it will take you there.
name: Riverbank

desc:
Huge rocks cover the ground. It seems easier to travel on the trail to the north
or on the river bank to the west. Steep mountains block your way east and south.
name: Rocky Flats

desc:
In a distance to the east the trail bends slightly to the north. Now it is
blocked by rocks torn down from the mountainsides by avalanches. The trail goes
later into a deep valley. Westwards the trail seems to lead you towards a dark
forest of pines. To the south are rocky flats.
name: Old Trail

desc:
The foot of the mountains appear to be only a mile away. But it is the size of
those mountains that plays you a trick. To the south is a riverbank. A branch of
the river Bruinen is floating westwards and later south of the dark forest far
west.
name: Old Trail

desc:
The trail passes a flat an rocky area. A few birches grows by the trail and you
are close to the mountains. The mountains in the horizon rise high up in the
sky. They are partly covered with snow. Wind makes mountain sides almost free
from snow throughout the year.
name: Old Trail

desc:
The river bends north-west here. In the south-east part of the bend huge amounts
of drift-wood have accumulated. Most of the drift-wood is rotten and you wonder
how long it takes for wood to rot in this cold climate.
name: Bend in the River Bruinen

desc:
The path continues on, winding along next to the river. The signs of a trade
route are still around, and there some flowers growing alongside the road.
Everything seems so peaceful around here.
name: Path to the Mountains

desc:
You stroll out into the plains, making your way through the grass and weeds.
Most of the plains lie to the southwest, and beyond them you can see a dark,
wildly growing forest. To the south are some gentle hills, and to the west is a
well worn trail. Beyond the trail lies the Bruinen.
name: Loudwater Plains

desc:
As you move farther into the plains, the trail becomes more distant and the
grass around you grows taller. There are many kinds of grasses and weeds growing
out here, as well as a number of the smaller wild animals. You are in the
northeast corner of the plains, as the plains stretch out to the southeast.
name: Loudwater Plains

desc:
The trail goes north-south here. To the west is the river Bruinen and to the
east is a dense vegetation you can't enter. The trail is overgrown and for sure
not often used these days. In the north is a crossing in the trail where you
must decide if you want to go further north or maybe east.
name: Trail

desc:
A few small bumps in the ground are beginning to form here, as the plains gently
roll into the hills to the south. The hills are not very large, in fact they are
barely hills, raising no more than a few feet off the ground. The plains
continue on to the north and to the west.
name: Loudwater Plains

desc:
The plains flow onward here. The tall grasses wave gently in the breeze and
small butterflies and dragonflies are flitting about oblivious to your presence.
The hills to the south continue on for a while, gently rising above the rest of
the plains.
name: Loudwater Plains

desc:
The plains start here, as the path winds it's way through them. The grasses here
are less sparse, and grow thicker as you walk out into the plains. The trail is
both to the north and the west, with some gentle hills rising to the the south.
More plains can be seen to the southwest and to the east.
name: Loudwater Plains

desc:
The river bends north-south here. You see small tunnels in the eastern river-
bank and can catch a glimpse of a muskrat. Fishes are swimming in the water and
insects are flying about.
name: The River Bruinen

desc:
The river bends south-east here. The water is ice cold and you can see small ice
floes drifting in the river. In the north part of the bend the river flows very
slowly. Water vegetation grows there and you see strange, little, green
creatures jumping from leaf to leaf.
name: Bend in the River Bruinen

desc:
The trail from the south splits here, with one fork leading north toward a
cliff, and the other heading toward the Misty Mountains. The trail is quite
overgrown and thick thorny bushes grow to either side.
name: Crossing in the Trail

desc:
The ford leads north-south over the Bruinen. The river is wider and calmer here
as in the east. The water is shallow enough to pass the river and reach the bank
on the other side. The river-bed is muddy and from time to time you trip over
rocks.
name: Ford in the River Bruinen

desc:
Turning west and north, this trail promises little but danger as it takes you
towards the mountains. Going back is not necessarily safe either, as the trail
is bordered by wilderness that only the strongest may survive in. To the south
and east, you can see a dark forest, from which various snarls and howls greet
you. Several pairs of eyes peer at you from that forest.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
Stretching east to west, this trail promises little but danger as it takes you
towards the mountains. Going back is not necessarily safe either, as the trail
is bordered by wilderness that only the strongest may survive in. To the south,
you can see a gloomy forest, from which various snarls and howls greet you. Soft
footfalls travel with you, emanating from the south.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
Stretching east to west, this trail promises little but danger as it takes you
towards the mountains. Going back is not necessarily safe either, as the trail
is bordered by wilderness that only the strongest may survive in. To the south,
you can see a murky forest, from which various snarls and howls greet you. The
sound of panting causes you to be alert. It must have come from the forest.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
Bending south and east, this trail promises little but danger as it takes you
towards the Hithaeglir Mountains. You dare not venture from the road, as about
this countryside there lies a wilderness that only the strongest or most cunning
could survive in. A small animal--perhaps a squirrel--darts away from the trail.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
Turning west and north, this trail promises little but danger as it takes you
towards the mountains. Going back is not necessarily safe either, as the trail
is bordered by wilderness that only the strongest may survive in. To the east,
you can see a murky forest, from which various snarls and howls greet you. The
cries of birds can also be heard from the forest.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
Stretching east to west, this trail promises little but danger as it takes you
towards the mountains. Going back is not necessarily safe either, as the trail
is bordered by wilderness that only the strongest may survive in. The vegetation
to the south is too thick to allow an egress that way, but to the north the
river borders the road, inviting you for a refreshing, if freezing, boat ride.
Your ears are soothed by the sound of water flowing past rocks, coming from the
river to the north.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
Stretching east to west, this trail promises little but danger as it takes you
towards the mountains. Going back is not necessarily safe either, as the trail
is bordered by wilderness that only the strongest may survive in. The vegetation
to the south is too thick to allow an egress that way, but to the north the
river borders the road, inviting you for a refreshing, if freezing, boat ride.
The whistle of the wind on the cliff-tops is shrill in your ears.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
Stretching east to west, this trail promises little but danger as it takes you
towards the mountains. Going back is not necessarily safe either, as the trail
is bordered by wilderness that only the strongest may survive in. The vegetation
to the south is too thick to allow an egress that way, but to the north the
river borders the road, inviting you for a refreshing, if freezing, boat ride. A
stiff wind blows through, and no cloak can keep out the chill that it brings.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
The trail runs east-west here. Wilderness surrounds the trail and to north you
can see a crossing in the Bruinen. The countryside is rough and wild and an icy
wind from the north chills your bones.
name: Trail to the Mountains

desc:
The main branch of the Bruinen meets here with a northern branch of the river.
Due to temperature differences in the two different branches turbulence arises.
name: Stream Crossing

desc:
In its last solitary moments, this river surges forward, past the broken lands
to the north and the trail to the south. In mere moments it will be joined,
removing its impurities and venturing to the healing lands of Elrond. This
accursed land will soon be far behind you, if you follow the river's wise path.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
Navigating between the rocky steppes to the north and the broad trail to the
south, the river relaxes, safe in the knowlege that soon it will be swimming
with its brother. But still the water is cold and clear, and it has not rid
itself of the bitter taint of the Hithaeglir. Perhaps it never will, and perhaps
it will never forget the torment that it and the hills around it suffered from
the orcs.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
Returning to face west, this river borders the seemingly well travelled trail to
the south. Almost condolingly, the waters wave goodbye to the trail, fearing for
its safety when it will trudge through the mountains, but glad that it will not
have to face that horror again. The current picks up speed at it comes nearer to
the warm lands of the south.
name: Tributary of the Bruinen

desc:
You marvel at how the pine trees of this forest could possibly withstand the
cold, and how they could survive in the vicious winds unbent. Still, the tops of
the trees are whipped about, and the very air is as chill as death. You pause a
second, looking around you, and see little signs of life, except the faint
claw-marks that tear up the forest floor.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
The forest has taken a more frantic, frightning aspect: every tree and every
leaf and every way looks exactly the same as every other. You cannot determine
which direction you are heading, or which direction you came from. The few
shafts of sunlight that reach the forest floor from the canopy above seem to
spin about you, and only confusion registers in your mind...
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
The murky shadows caused by the forest surround you, in every direction, not
more than ten meters away the sunlight is almost completely closed off from the
forest. Peering intently into the shadows, you can just make out a form darting
quickly away...and several other shadowy figures grouping together, eyes glowing
in the twilight.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
The forest has taken a more frantic, frightning aspect: every tree and every
leaf and every way looks exactly the same as every other. You cannot determine
which direction you are heading, or which direction you came from. The few
shafts of sunlight that reach the forest floor from the canopy above seem to
spin about you, and only confusion registers in your mind...
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
The forest has taken a more frantic, frightning aspect: every tree and every
leaf and every way looks exactly the same as every other. You cannot determine
which direction you are heading, or which direction you came from. The few
shafts of sunlight that reach the forest floor from the canopy above seem to
spin about you, and only confusion registers in your mind...
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
The forest you stand in is thick, the trees clump together for protection
against the cold. Occasionally a fierce wind reminds you that you, too, need
shelter from this clime. The ground is littered with dead and dying pine
needles, crunching softly as you walk. Sunlight is blocked from the canopy of
trees overhead, and you must strain your eyes to see more than several meters
ahead, all the forms and trees and plants and animals meld into one confusing
scene in the dark. The quick panting of breath can be heard.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
A frigid wind bites into your face as it howls through the cluttered, cold
branches. Old pine trees rise like black slender manilantles all around,
gathered for some clandestine, and horrible purpose. The ground is cold and
hard, and sharpened rocks and hardened roots protrude from the shadows. The wind
rises again, and this time seems to be mingled with a wolf's lonely call.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
The climbing gets slightly harder here, and the rough slope continues to ascend
to the east. The chill wind blows against your skin and hair, making you feel
much colder than you should.
name: Low Hills

desc:
This hill rises up above its surroundings, cresting the mounds about it like
some ancient tower of old. In all directions the view is breathtaking, and the
only sound that can be heard is the whistle of the cold wind that bites through
even the thickest clothing. And disturbing this are the howl of what you hope
are only wolves.
name: The High Hill

desc:
The forest has taken a more frantic, frightning aspect: every tree and every
leaf and every way looks exactly the same as every other. You cannot determine
which direction you are heading, or which direction you came from. The few
shafts of sunlight that reach the forest floor from the canopy above seem to
spin about you, and only confusion registers in your mind...
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
The ground breaks up slightly, growing more rocky and steeper to the south. But
still the gloom covers the forest, and you cannot see what the land turns into
as it ventures southward. However, you do see that in all directions the forest
seems to continue unabated and perpetually.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
The forest you stand in is thick, the trees clump together for protection
against the cold. Occasionally a fierce wind reminds you that you, too, need
shelter from this clime. The ground is littered with dead and dying pine
needles, crunching softly as you walk. Sunlight is blocked from the canopy of
trees overhead, and you must strain your eyes to see more than several meters
ahead, all the forms and trees and plants and animals meld into one confusing
scene in the dark. The quick panting of breath can be heard.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
The forest you stand in is thick, the trees clump together for protection
against the cold. Occasionally a fierce wind reminds you that you, too, need
shelter from this clime. The ground is littered with dead and dying pine
needles, crunching softly as you walk. Sunlight is blocked from the canopy of
trees overhead, and you must strain your eyes to see more than several meters
ahead, all the forms and trees and plants and animals meld into one confusing
scene in the dark. The quick panting of breath can be heard.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
You follow a well-used animal trail and then lose it, stumbling back into the
undergrowth, and scratching your face and skin. Wolves howl balefully into the
dark, first from in front, and then another some distance behind. The immense
old pines block sight in a haphazard fashion, and you seem to catch sight of
danger several times, but are never sure of it.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
The trees grow particularly close together here, in a thick clump that takes
some doing to get around. To the south, you can barely see some hills through
the wall of pines. And all around lies the forest, waiting, it seems, for
something.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
Here is the edge of a large pine forest. The fringe is not so dark or cold as
the interior, but still the dank and frigid mist wafts dormantly around. The
sounds of a living, breathing forest quietly unnerve and disturb you.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
Darkness descends upon you, but no stars speckle the black mantle overhead. It
is a kind of thick, oppressive blackness, more like the midnight of a cavern
than that of the surface lands. A dank mist circulates through the chilly air,
infecting you, no doubt, with all sorts of unhealthy vapours. The sounds of
something pacing through the undergrowth echo preternaturally from deeper within
the woods.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
Without warning, the darkness suddenly penetrates your heart, and you feel
fear--fear of the wild creatures half-seen in the periphery, fear of losing your
way in this horrid wood, and fear of the next step. You maneouver through
half-frozen pines as they look down impassively at your plight.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
You stand in the fringes of a deep, cold pine forest. The thick canopy envelopes
the forest in an eternal twilight, with only small patches of illumination. The
ground crunches softly under your feet as you step among the fallen brown
needles.
name: Pine-Forest

desc:
The climbing gets slightly harder here, and the rough slope continues to ascend
to the south. The chill wind blows against your skin and hair, making you feel
much colder than you should.
name: Low Hills

desc:
The hills border a deep pine-forest to the north, and continue to the east. West
lies a particularly large hill, overlooking the bleak countryside like an
enormous turret, built long ago by the armies of men, and left to sink back into
its basic elements.
name: Low Hills

desc:
A particularly hardy variety of short, sparse grass covers the hills here,
blasted by wind and sleet at least half of the year. The hills here are short
and almost pleasant to traverse, but continuing east, they become high and
broken. The edge of a huge pine forest lies to the north.
name: Low Hills

desc:
Grey, rolling hills are all around, with only a few stunted trees to relieve the
monotony. Here the winds seem to blow quite harshly, pressing the dry scratchy
grass against the earth. The scene slowly flattens out and gives way to a pine
forest.
name: Hills

desc:
It seems these hills go on for miles and miles. The mounds are regular and
boring, but in their lack of identity somehow threatening. Each one could hide a
servant of evil, or cover a treasure or undead horror. So you travel with
constant, nervous alertness, among the barren grey hills, seemingly empty, but
haunted with shadows of danger.
name: Hills

desc:
Here a path, almost obliterated by the harsh elements of the north, begins and
heads through some hills, to the mountains of the east. These rising hills are
gloomy and grey, and there is no movement or life that you can discern.
name: Rising Hills

desc:
You notice that these hills, ranging from tall to very short, all seem to flow
in a common direction. The maze-like enclosure of their sometimes-rolling,
sometimes-vertical walls zig-zags back and forth. But all the way across, the
elevation increases quickly as you travel to the east.
name: Rising Hills

desc:
High, smooth hills undulate all around, and surge upward to meet a dismal range
of mountains to the east. The ground is cold and ungiving, the few bushes small,
hardy, unloved things. A scarcely-used path goes from east to west, following
the rise and fall of the land.
name: Rising Hills

desc:
A quiet little cleared space leads to more ruins. Although this site is
overgrown with bushes and small trees, it is apparent that once a village
huddled here beneath the pines. All that is left above-ground is a half- fallen
wall of stone and a couple scattered white marble blocks.
name: Overgrown Ruins

desc:
Once a short, sturdy rock gate guarded the entrance to the village, but it has
long since deteriorated into rubble. The walls are broken and covered with dark
lichens, and extend into the shadows of the forest on either side. Beyond the
gate lie the ruins of a humble little village, bathed in the cold darkness of
the pines.
name: Crumbling Gate

desc:
A narrow path runs along the forest floor, eternally shaded by the thick opaque
canopy. A mostly-intact stone hut opens up to the path, but the darkened
interior is hidden from casual inspection.
name: Dark Path

desc:
The path continues through the remains of the village. Most of the buildings
seem to have rotted away long ago, but several edifices still stand in stony
defiance of the elements and the wind.
name: Dark Path

desc:
This building, which seems to have once been a little guardshack, is one of the
better-preserved in the village, and now serves as at least a part-time
residence of a bevy of swallows. The roof is fairly solid, and three of the four
walls still stand. The hut is dark inside.
name: Broken-down Guardshack

desc:
A cobblestone road is set in a track to the north. Some kind of change seems to
have occurred in the last couple of decades, as the cobbles are sunk several
feet from their original position, and partially covered with damp, cold loam.
Off to the west a narrow, crumbly path ascends steeply up the mountain.
name: Sunken Cobbles

desc:
This bumpy alley runs between two overgrown lots, filled with knee-high grass
and thick, short bushes. You step over a large, fallen tree trunk, broken open
and rotten inside.
name: Alley

desc:
You stand in an open alley that connects the two main sections of the village,
or at least the parts that still stand. To either side is a scraggly field, that
probably contained a bunch of huts at one time. A stone structure lies to the
east.
name: Alley

desc:
One stands in front of a smallish, round structure, constructed of dozens of
head-sized stones fitted together into a dome-shape. Not a single building block
has fallen, despite the obvious age of the building. In its youth it probably
stood about three feet higher, because now it has sunken into the black earth
and has covered part of the door which marked the front.
name: Before the Sunken Stonehut

desc:
You stand on a rocky, shallow hill. A narrow trail leads up the mountainside,
where little rocks keep dislodging themselves and tumbling down. High above you
can barely see a thick, squat tower of some sort, half-hidden by a moss-covered
crag.
name: The Foot of the Hill

desc:
This trail leads up and around the mountain, heading in a northerly direction.
Shaded by the tops of the pines, and hidden by large granite outcroppings, it
would be hard to spot from below.
name: A Trail up the Mountainside

desc:
The mountain wall and the outcropping on the right almost meet on the top,
throwing darkness between, and giving the impression of a long, tall tunnel. You
can just barely see the sky above, cluttered with some silhouetted bushes that
shudder in the wind.
name: Old Ledge

desc:
You stand before a rude, squat tower; round and built of rough stones, with
earth heaped around the greater part of its circumference. The tower is about
twenty feet high, and leans against the mountainside. A rusty old irondoor is
sunk into the lime-kiln, like the door of a great oven. A path leads up around
the side of the kiln, to the top of the structure.
name: Base of the Lime-Kiln

desc:
The inside of the kiln is wide and somewhat more expansive than it would seem.
The stones here are blackened and melted from the great heat that once blasted
them. Now, they are mossy and dark for a different reason. The wind whistles
over the top of the kiln, but not overly loud. The interior is cool and
sheltered from most exposure. Embedded in the south wall is a sturdy-looking
iron door, but no handle is apparent on this side.
name: In the Kiln

desc:
You stand on top of the tower-like structure; from here you can see all around.
To the southwest, a dark pine-forest covers the land like a heavy rug, and the
Misty Mountains rise up everywhere else. Directly below you see the layout of an
ancient, ruined village, long since decomposed into the earth. Only a few stone
buildings remain to attest to its existence. But the old plots are still free of
trees, and from up here the traces of the village are quite clear. Beyond the
village proper you see some black caves dotting the rock face. Underfoot is the
open top of the kiln, some eight feet across, and ten deep. Inside, it is dark
and cool, and the stones are black with the lichens and mosses of years of long
disuse.
name: Top of Kiln

desc:
Piles of earth and stone embrace the kiln, covering it to the depth of a few
feet. This path leads to the top of the kiln.
name: Rocky Hillock

desc:
Something lives here from the looks of this place. There are big footprints
everywhere. Piles of ash-covered bones are scattered about. Whatever lives here
must live off of unlucky wanderers like you. The only way out of here is west
and you think you had better head in that direction right now.
name: Back of the Burned Forest

desc:
Fear settles down on top of you, and you hardly notice it. You have suddenly
become sure that someone or something is tracking you through this wasted land.
You can escape to your south or head even deeper into the forest to the east.
South looks like a better choice.
name: Deep in the Burned Forest

desc:
You are now deep in this awful forest. The dust and ashes that rise up as you
trample through are beginning to choke you. All the blackness around you seems
to suck up the sunlight. You don't think anything could live in this place, but
you aren't so sure when you hear grunting noises nearby.
name: Centre of the Burned Forest

desc:
Tracks of some humanoid creature go through the ashes here, and they look fresh.
Though the trees are the only things that obstruct your view, they are so thick
that you can't see far. The ashes that you are kicking up also make it hard to
see. It's strange, you think, that the ashes of a fire that seems to have
happened so long ago have not been washed away yet.
name: Burned Forest

desc:
It seems that you have hit a dead end in your wanderings. The trees to the east
and west of you, though burned, are still standing tall and strong. Sometimes it
seems like they aren't really dead. South of you is a steep cliff wall that is
obviously impassable.
name: Burned Forest

desc:
As you enter the hidden cavern you shiver, it is cold and damp in here. You hear
a faint sound of water running down the rocks at the other side of the cavern.
Some small bugs rapidly run across the rocks and disappears again. The only
visible exit out of this cave is through a hole in the rocks to the west.
name: Hidden Cavern

desc:
A small hill rises up in the forest here, blocked off in all directions except
back to the east. There is little vegetation on this hill, as it is covered
mainly in rocks and pebbles. There are a number of bones scattered about the
hill.
name: A Rocky Hill in the Wild Forest

desc:
This part of the burrow has been taken over by something a little less than
benevolent. Where the other part was at least clean, whatever lives here just
throws the remains of its meals into the corner. Joining the smell of wet fur is
the cloying smell of the decaying remains in the corner. The burrow ends here,
and the only way out is back to the west.
name: Deep Burrow

desc:
You reach the peak of this small group of hills. Visions of glory and fortune
enter your head as you look around you to the majestic plains, the wild forests,
and the mysterious mountains. The hill slopes down to the west and to the east.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
It is a very strange cave. Magical powers surround you. Perhaps this cave was a
meeting place where magic users in old days created new spells. Some years ago
the cave was flooded by the nearby river, and new inhabitants moved in here.
name: Cave

desc:
The dense vegetation of dark trees covers hills to the north and east. To the
south and southwest there is a large dark forest. To the west are the only flats
