in this area. Northwest rolling hills makes the landscape smoother.
name: Dark Hill

desc:
This flatland borders the verdant forest to the south. Few trees grow here, only
a few hardy birches. It does however give you a good view, allowing you to spot
potential enemies a mile away, or they you.
name: Flats

desc:
You know that the ground is almost one hundred and fifty feet below, but you
can't see it. Branches block the view down. From here, in the southeastern part
of the forest, you have a marvellous overlook on a clear day. The heart of the
forest is to the west. East, at some distance, are two of river Bruinen's
contributors. They meet and run southwards. The river disappears into a deep
valley far to the southwest maybe the valley of Rivendell, or Imladris in elven
tongue, signifying deep-dale-cleft. Close to the river there is a trail.
name: In a Tree

desc:
You are in one of the big trees a good climber can reach. Another tree is to the
north. The thick branch pointing that direction seem stable enough. Dense leaves
block out the sky. This place is left in eternal darkness.
name: In a Tree

desc:
The air is damp. A wall of branches and leaves does not allow any light to enter
here. The only exits are down and south to another tree. It looks easy to climb
down but it might be hard or impossible to come back here that way.
name: In a Tree

desc:
You are standing in front of a small lodge to the south. North is the river
Bruinen, hidden behind a grassy slope. Another slope, to the west, leads to a
high plateau overlooking the surroundings. To the east, you see a hill.
name: The Small Spring

desc:
A rather steep grassy slope carries you up to some high cliffs extending north,
over the river. You can reach the riverside by going down, to the east. From the
south, you hear the sound of water running in a small spring in front of a
wooden lodge.
name: Sloping Grounds

desc:
You are standing in the middle of a grassy sea. Vast plains stretches in all
directions, and you see nothing exciting anywhere. Only to the east, something
disturbs the flat view - the Misty Mountains towering over the world, hiding
it's secrets.
name: Vast Plains

desc:
Rocks fill these plains, and make it very hard to move. To the east, you see a
black hole, extending into a hill. Dare you enter to check what that snoring
sound comes from? Turning to the south, you notice a small, nearly overgrown
trail leading east and west. West and north is nothing but vast plains.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
What is this? You have found a very dark and narrow tunnel leading east. You
suddenly notice that you have no possibility what so ever to turn back west -
the only way to go seems to be on. Making your weapon ready for any unexpected
encounters, you crawl on...
name: Narrow Tunnel

desc:
You have found a small hill, not reachable from any direction except the small
tunnel to the west. Something gives you a feeling that this place had better be
left undisturbed. As you look around, to see what the origin of that particular
feeling is, you see a small stone standing on the hill. Thick bushes and rocks
stops you from moving north, east and south.
name: A Lonely Hill

desc:
You have no idea what or who that might have created this small cave. Small bugs
swiftly scurry across the rocky ground as you enter. At the back of the cave the
roof slowly slopes down to meet with the bottom of the cave. During bad weather
this place could give some shelter to the tired adventurer. The only way out
seems to be the same way as you entered from under the bridge.
name: Cave under the Bridge

desc:
There are a lot of small rocks on this hill. You can look down at a waterfall.
Your eyes can follow the river's route through the terrain. The river joins
another river very far away.
name: Steep Hill

desc:
You are in a crevice between a large mountain to the east and a smaller one to
the west. Sounds of running water comes from west and north. The crevice
continuse northward and opens up southward to an old trail.
name: Steep Mountain above a Shallow and Rocky River

desc:
Inside, a single burnt out torch on the far wall and a pile of leaves and straw
are all that give indications to its occupancy. A campfire has painted the
mountain walls black with ashes and soot. It is humid and the sound of dripping
water can be heard in a distance. There is a paper in one dusty corner.
name: A Damp Cave

desc:
There is no path through this dark forest, but a lack of ground vegetation makes
travel fairly easy. Dense pines block out nearly all natural light from this
area. Now and then sounds echo between the trunks, but they are not from birds.
name: Dark Pine Forest

desc:
The dark forest meets a river and you can hear the sound of running water from
the east. On the opposite side of the river there is an old, seldom trodden
trail which seems to lead into the Mountains. Maybe it may lead travellers to a
pass.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
You have a beautiful overview of the lands around when there is no fog. To the
east there are the mountains of Hithaeglir, the Misty Mountains. Southwards the
mountains go on past the horizon and east of them is the old kingdom of Rhudaur.
Between the mountains there are flat and rocky areas, hills covered by trees and
dark forests.
name: Top of the Hill

desc:
Here is a small, cosy hole that some animal made deep within the forest. The
hole is warm, dry, and clean of any twigs or needles. There is a distinct smell
of some kind of animal, but you don't know which. You worry about being in the
hole when the residents are here.
name: Cubbyhole
